mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and a decline in retail sales volume indicates synchronized demand weakness across durable and non-durable goods sectors, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high industrial production growth, rising capacity utilization eventually triggers supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high capacity utilization
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates positively with forward equity returns during high utilization phases, or the forward return differential between high and low utilization regimes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding the historical trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above trend consistently coincides with negative or flat equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days, yielding a negative Rank IC across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average return on equity, forcing multiple compression and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when the funds rate exceeds 5.0%, or the forward return spread between high and low rate regimes shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that eventually forces wage inflation, eroding corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising employment and falling unemployment fails to predict negative equity returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase where real GDP growth accelerates while inflation remains subdued, equity valuations expand rapidly as earnings revisions turn positive, generating strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Accelerating GDP growth combined with stable inflation consistently produces negative or flat equity returns, or the predictive power of the regime-conditioned signal drops below a Rank IC of 0.04 over any 2-year period.
